summary: Uses too few procedure steps to verify minimum workflow coverage. [CONFIG]

frontmatter.name: short-procedure-review
frontmatter.description: Use when validating that underspecified skill procedures are rejected.

guiding_principle: A designed skill must include enough ordered steps to make execution auditable. [DOC]
quality_criteria: Each step has input and output fields. [CODE]
The expected failure is the step count. [CODE]
anti_patterns: Collapsing discovery, validation, and reporting into one step.
Omitting the input for a procedure step.
Omitting the output for a procedure step.
Letting prose replace an ordered procedure.

edge_cases.case: The skill has only three steps.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Reject the procedure as below minimum. [CODE]
Do not mask the procedure error. [CODE]

validation: This fixture must fail on procedure length. [CODE]
risks: If accepted, underspecified skill designs could pass review. [ASSUMPTION]
